skill.name: design-taste-frontend
skill.description: Senior UI/UX Engineer. Architect digital interfaces overriding default LLM biases. Enforces metric-based rules, strict component architecture, CSS hardware acceleration, and balanced design engineering.
skill.summary: AI design skill that enforces premium frontend patterns, eliminates generic AI output, and guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce high-quality interfaces.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: All 161 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The external_commands pattern (125 locations) is markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution. The blocker weak-crypto findings (16 locations) are misidentified hex color values in CSS. The system-reconnaissance findings (20 locations) are legitimate AI coding instructions for dependency verification. The single network finding is a placeholder image URL for UI mockups. The skill is a markdown instruction document for AI coding assistants with no executable code, no network calls, and no security risks.

content.user_title: Build premium interfaces with design guardrails
content.value_statement: AI coding assistants produce generic, predictable interfaces by default. This skill enforces strict design rules, component architecture, and performance standards to generate professional-quality frontend code.
actual_capabilities: Enforce consistent typography, color, and layout rules across generated interfaces
Guide AI to use proper React Server Components and client component isolation
Generate high-performance animations using CSS transforms and hardware acceleration
Prevent common AI design anti-patterns such as centered-hero templates and purple gradients
Provide structured bento-grid architectures with perpetual micro-interactions
Validate Tailwind CSS version compatibility and configuration before code generation
limitations: This skill provides instructions only. It does not execute code or install packages.
Design rules target React and Next.js projects. Other frameworks may not align with the guidance.
Motion and animation patterns assume Framer Motion availability. Pure CSS alternatives are not provided.
The skill does not generate actual designs. It instructs the AI assistant on how to construct interfaces.

use_cases.title: Professional SaaS dashboard development
use_cases.description: A frontend developer uses this skill with Claude Code to generate a polished SaaS dashboard with bento-grid layouts, proper loading states, and smooth layout transitions without manual design decisions.

use_cases.title: Design-system consistency across teams
use_cases.description: A design engineer shares this skill with their team to ensure all AI-generated components follow the same typography scale, color calibration, and responsive breakpoint rules.

use_cases.title: High-end marketing landing pages
use_cases.description: A solo founder uses this skill with Codex to produce an asymmetric hero section, kinetic typography, and scroll-driven animations that avoid generic AI aesthetics.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a basic landing page section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hero section for a SaaS product with a left-aligned headline, a supporting paragraph, and a call-to-action button. Use the design baseline from this skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: You need a clean starting point for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Build a responsive card grid
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a feature section using an asymmetric two-column zig-zag layout. Alternate text content with image placeholders. Follow the anti-center-bias and grid-over-flex-math rules.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: You want to avoid the generic three-card row pattern

prompt_templates.title: Create an animated bento-grid dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a five-card bento grid with perpetual micro-interactions. Each card must have an infinite loop animation using Framer Motion spring physics. Include layout transitions and staggered orchestration for list items.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: You need a production-ready dashboard with motion

prompt_templates.title: Implement liquid-glass UI with performance guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a glassmorphism panel with inner border refraction and tinted shadows. Apply grain filter only to a fixed pseudo-element. Ensure all animations use transform and opacity exclusively for hardware acceleration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: You want premium visual effects without performance regression

output_examples.input: Create a hero section with asymmetric layout and premium typography
output_examples.output: A left-aligned hero section using min-h-[100dvh] with a text-6xl heading in Geist font, a text-gray-600 paragraph with max-w-[65ch], and a call-to-action button with active scale-[0.98] feedback. The right half contains a subtle background image with a fade gradient.

output_examples.input: Build a bento grid with five animated cards
output_examples.output: A responsive grid with five cards: an auto-sorting task list with layoutId swaps, a search bar with typewriter cycling, a live status panel with breathing indicators, an infinite data carousel, and a focus-mode document view with staggered highlights. All cards use Framer Motion spring physics and are memoized for performance.

best_practices: Always check package.json before suggesting third-party library installation commands.
Use min-h-[100dvh] instead of h-screen for full-height sections to prevent mobile layout jumps.
Isolate interactive components as leaf components with use client at the top. Keep server components static.
Animate only transform and opacity properties. Never animate top, left, width, or height.
anti_patterns: Using centered hero sections with purple gradients. This is the most common AI design cliché.
Building complex percentage-based layouts with flexbox math instead of CSS Grid.
Placing perpetual animations inside parent components. This triggers unnecessary re-renders and performance collapse.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install it in any AI coding assistant that supports custom skills.

faq.question: Does this skill require a specific framework?
faq.answer: The default architecture targets React and Next.js with Tailwind CSS. The design principles apply broadly, but code patterns assume these tools.

faq.question: Can I override the baseline design values?
faq.answer: Yes. The baseline values for DESIGN_VARIANCE, MOTION_INTENSITY, and VISUAL_DENSITY are starting points. Always adapt them when the user requests different settings.

faq.question: Does this skill install packages or run code?
faq.answer: No. This skill contains instructions only. It may suggest npm install commands, but the AI assistant executes those actions in your project.

faq.question: Why are emojis banned in this skill?
faq.answer: Emojis render inconsistently across platforms and reduce the professional quality of interfaces. Use SVG icons from Phosphor or Radix instead.

faq.question: How do I add this skill to my AI assistant?
faq.answer: Copy the SKILL.md file into your assistant skill directory. The assistant will apply these rules automatically during code generation.
